Antenna.rdfs:label: Antenna
Antenna.skos:definition: Transducer converting guided electromagnetic waves to free-space propagating waves (TX) and vice versa (RX). The antenna determines beam pattern, gain, scan coverage, and polarization.
Antenna.pwmo:purpose: Transmit high-power RF pulses into space; receive reflected energy; provide directional gain; determine angular resolution

gain_dBi.description: Peak boresight gain relative to isotropic radiator. Typical: 25-45 dBi

beamwidth_3dB_az_deg.description: 3 dB azimuth beamwidth. Determines azimuth resolution

sidelobe_level_dB.description: Peak sidelobe level relative to main beam. Low sidelobes critical for ECCM. Typical: -25 to -45 dB

scan_coverage_az_deg.description: Electronic scan coverage in azimuth (phased arrays)

ParabolicReflector.rdfs:label: Parabolic Reflector Antenna
ParabolicReflector.skos:definition: Single or dual reflector antenna focusing beam via parabolic surface geometry. Classic radar antenna.
pwmo:disadvantages: Large physical size for low frequencies

SlottedWaveguideArray.rdfs:label: Slotted Waveguide Array
SlottedWaveguideArray.skos:definition: Linear or planar array of resonant slots cut in waveguide walls, acting as radiating elements. Used in airborne and naval search radars for low-profile, high-gain flat antenna.

PESA_Array.rdfs:label: Passive Electronically Scanned Array
PESA_Array.skos:definition: Array of radiating elements with individually controllable phase shifters, fed from single central transmitter via corporate or series feed network.

pwmo:phaseShifterTypes.PIN_Diode: Fast switching (<1μs), moderate power, higher loss
pwmo:phaseShifterTypes.MEMS: Very low loss, moderate speed, emerging technology

PESA_Array.pwmo:grating_lobe_avoidance: Element spacing ≤ λ/2 for grating lobe free scanning ±90°

AESA_Array.rdfs:label: Active Electronically Scanned Array
AESA_Array.skos:definition: Phased array where each radiating element (or sub-array) has its own integrated TR module containing PA, LNA, phase shifter, and attenuator. All modules operate coherently.

pwmo:digital_beamforming.description: Digital beamforming (DBF) performs beam steering in digital domain after ADC at element level, enabling simultaneous multiple independent beams
pwmo:digital_beamforming.requirement: One ADC/DAC per element or sub-array

ConformalArray.rdfs:label: Conformal Antenna Array
ConformalArray.skos:definition: Phased array whose radiating surface conforms to the shape of the aircraft fuselage, wing leading edge, or other structure, minimizing drag and radar cross-section.
pwmo:challenges: Element pattern variation across curved surface

SparseArray.rdfs:label: Sparse / Thinned Array
SparseArray.skos:definition: Array with non-uniform element spacing (fewer elements than a filled λ/2-spaced array) designed to reduce cost while maintaining aperture size and main beam width at cost of higher sidelobe levels.

Transmitter.rdfs:label: Radar Transmitter
Transmitter.skos:definition: Generates high-power RF energy for radiation. May be a single high-power device (PESA/mechanical) or distributed across TR modules (AESA).

peak_power_kW.description: Peak instantaneous output power. Range: 1kW (airborne) to 10MW (BMEWS)

duty_cycle_percent.description: Average/Peak power ratio * 100. CW = 100%, typical pulsed = 0.1-20%

bandwidth_MHz.description: Instantaneous bandwidth. Determines range resolution after compression

Magnetron.skos:definition: Crossed-field vacuum tube oscillator generating high-power microwave energy. Self-oscillating (not coherent). Used in early and simpler radars.

Klystron.skos:definition: Linear beam vacuum tube amplifier providing high power, stable, coherent output. Used in high-power ground and naval radars.

TravelingWaveTube_TWT.skos:definition: Wideband vacuum tube amplifier with electron beam interacting with slow-wave structure. Provides high power with wide bandwidth — common in airborne radars before AESA.

SolidStatePowerAmplifier_SSPA.skos:definition: Transistor-based RF power amplifier. In AESA, each TR module contains one SSPA. Technology transition: GaAs → GaN (current generation).

pwmo:GaN_MMIC.thermal_conductivity_advantage: GaN on SiC substrate: 3.5x better thermal conductivity vs GaAs

Receiver.rdfs:label: Radar Receiver
Receiver.skos:definition: Captures extremely weak return signals from targets and prepares them for signal processing. Performance defined by noise figure, dynamic range, and sensitivity.

noise_figure_dB.description: Ratio of SNR at input to SNR at output. Lower is better. Typical: 2-8 dB

dynamic_range_dB.description: Ratio of maximum non-saturating signal to minimum detectable. Typical: 80-120 dB

Superheterodyne.description: Classical architecture: LNA → mixer (down-convert to IF) → IF filter/amplifier → ADC
Superheterodyne.advantage: Mature, high performance, good image rejection

DirectConversion.description: LNA → IQ mixer (down-convert to baseband) → ADC

DirectSampling.description: High-speed ADC samples directly at RF; digital down-conversion in FPGA/DSP
DirectSampling.requirement: ADC sampling rate > 2x RF frequency (or sub-Nyquist bandpass sampling)

LNA.rdfs:label: Low Noise Amplifier
LNA.skos:definition: First active stage after antenna; must add minimum noise. Sets receiver noise figure. GaAs or InP HEMT technology.
LNA.pwmo:purpose: Amplify weak received signal before it travels through lossy cable/filter where noise would be added

Limiter.skos:definition: Protects LNA from damage during transmit pulse leakage. PIN diode or ferrite limiter.
Limiter.pwmo:purpose: Protection of sensitive receive chain from own transmitter pulse and nearby radar interference

Circulator.skos:definition: Ferrite device that routes TX power to antenna and RX power from antenna to receiver; provides isolation between TX and RX paths in shared-aperture systems.

Duplexer.skos:definition: T/R switch or passive device separating transmit and receive paths sharing single antenna port. Can be electromechanical, PIN diode, or ferrite.

Mixer.skos:definition: Frequency conversion device multiplying RF signal with local oscillator to produce IF signal. Diode or FET-based.

FrequencySynthesizer.rdfs:label: Frequency Synthesizer / Exciter
FrequencySynthesizer.skos:definition: Generates all coherent frequency references for the radar: transmit waveform, local oscillator, ADC clock, timing signals. The heart of coherent radar processing.

phase_noise_dBc_Hz.description: Phase noise at given offset; limits clutter cancellation capability. Typical: -130 to -160 dBc/Hz at 1 kHz offset

frequency_agility_hopping_rate_Hz.description: Pulse-to-pulse frequency change rate for LPI/ECCM

FrequencySynthesizer.pwmo:waveformGeneration: Modern synthesizers generate arbitrary waveforms digitally (DAC-based) enabling LFM chirp, FMCW, stepped frequency, OFDM, and pseudo-random waveforms

SignalProcessor.rdfs:label: Signal Processor
SignalProcessor.skos:definition: Performs all digital processing of received radar data from raw ADC samples through to detected target reports. Implements the complete signal processing chain.

FPGA.role: High-speed deterministic processing: pulse compression, FFT, CFAR, beamforming

GPU.role: Parallel floating-point computation: SAR imaging, STAP, AI inference

DSP.role: Fixed-point or floating-point signal processing: filtering, FFT, tracking

GPGPU.role: General-purpose GPU for AI/ML inference and complex signal processing

CustomASIC.role: Maximum throughput at minimum power for specific algorithms (beamforming, FFT)

pwmo:processingChain: Moving Target Indicator (MTI) / Moving Target Detection (MTD)

IFF_System.rdfs:label: Identification Friend or Foe (IFF)
IFF_System.skos:definition: Secondary radar system using coded interrogations and transponder replies to distinguish friendly from unknown/hostile targets. Integrated with primary radar to correlate primary track with IFF return.

pwmo:modes.Mode_2: Military only; 4096 code combinations; unit identification
pwmo:modes.Mode_4: Military crypto; IFF challenge-response with KIT-1C crypto keys
pwmo:modes.Mode_5: Current standard NATO IFF; encrypted; GPS position inclusion; Level 1 and Level 2; ADS-B compatible
pwmo:modes.Mode_S: Civil selective addressing; 24-bit ICAO aircraft address
pwmo:modes.ADS-B: Automatic Dependent Surveillance Broadcast; GPS position broadcast

IFF_System.pwmo:militaryCritical: Mode 5 crypto keys must be loaded daily; failure creates IFF blue-on-blue risk

PowerSupply.rdfs:label: Power Supply / Power Conditioning
PowerSupply.skos:definition: Converts prime power (AC mains, generator, vehicle bus) to regulated voltages required by radar subsystems. High-power radars require significant power infrastructure.

HighVoltagePSU.purpose: Powers vacuum tubes (klystron, TWT): 10-150 kV, high current

ModulatorPSU.purpose: Generates high-power transmit pulses; energy storage in pulse-forming network (PFN) or capacitor bank

UPS.purpose: Uninterruptible power supply; battery backup for graceful shutdown

CoolingSystem.rdfs:label: Thermal Management / Cooling System
CoolingSystem.skos:definition: Removes waste heat from power amplifiers, TR modules, and electronics. Critical for GaN-based AESA where power densities exceed 10 W/mm². Insufficient cooling causes performance degradation and premature failure.

LiquidCooling.application: GaN AESA TR modules, high-power amplifiers

MicrochannelCooling.description: Liquid cooling channels fabricated directly under GaN substrate

ThermoelectricCooling_TEC.application: Spot cooling of low-noise amplifiers and detectors

HeatPipe.application: Passive heat spreading in space and airborne systems

pwmo:thermalDesignPrinciples: Junction temperature must stay <150°C for GaN
Thermal resistance minimization path: junction → package → cold plate → coolant
Thermal cycling causes solder joint fatigue

MissionComputer.rdfs:label: Mission / Radar Management Computer
MissionComputer.skos:definition: High-level control computer managing radar modes, scheduling, resource management, operator interface, data fusion, and external communications. Above signal processor in processing hierarchy.

RotaryJoint.rdfs:label: Rotary Joint
RotaryJoint.skos:definition: Waveguide or coaxial device allowing RF energy transmission from stationary feed to rotating antenna structure without interruption. Critical wear component in rotating radar antennas.

INS_GPS.rdfs:label: Navigation System (INS/GPS)
INS_GPS.skos:definition: Provides radar platform position, velocity, and attitude. Essential for SAR processing (motion compensation), airborne radar stabilization, and geo-referenced target reporting.

INS_GPS_coupled.advantage: INS bridges GPS outages; GPS corrects INS drift

DataLink.rdfs:label: Data Link / Communication Interface
DataLink.skos:definition: Transmits radar track data, raw video, and control messages to/from C2 systems, other sensors, and weapon systems. Enables networked radar operation.

JTIDS.note: Joint Tactical Information Distribution System; carries Link 16

MIDS.note: Multifunctional Information Distribution System; miniaturized Link 16

Link_11.note: Legacy; being phased out by Link 22

Link_22.note: NATO Link 22; HF and UHF; replaces Link 11

SADL.note: Situational Awareness Data Link; USAF intra-flight

IFDL.note: Intra-Flight Data Link; F-22 low-probability intercept

MADL.note: Multifunction Advanced Data Link; F-35 LPI; 1 Gbps encrypted

TTNT.note: Tactical Targeting Network Technology; high-throughput IP-based

CDL.note: Common Data Link; SIGINT/SAR imagery downlink; 10-137 Mbps

BuiltInTest_BIT.rdfs:label: Built-In Test (BIT) System
BuiltInTest_BIT.skos:definition: Continuous or initiated self-test capability monitoring radar health, detecting faults, and isolating failures to line-replaceable unit (LRU) level to support rapid maintenance.

pwmo:types.IBIT: Initiated BIT; manual trigger; comprehensive test
pwmo:types.CBIT: Continuous BIT; ongoing monitoring during operation
pwmo:types.PBIT: Power-up BIT; executed at system startup

BuiltInTest_BIT.pwmo:faultIsolation: Target: isolate to LRU within 10-30 minutes by maintenance technician
